danger_signals.visual_form: Screen flash/fade to battle background; enemy sprite appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; player safety compromised

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened tile palettes, swamp-like or barren ground textures
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous wilderness zone; high monster encounter rate

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, menacing creature designs; red/dark color schemes on enemies
Dark stone architecture, narrow corridors, shadowed interior
danger_signals.meaning: Boss territory or heavily guarded hostile location

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite trembling or with distressed posture
danger_signals.meaning: Nearby threat or dangerous situation in region

safety_signals.visual_form: Lighter tile palettes, buildings with doors, NPCs standing calmly
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; shops, healing, rest available

safety_signals.visual_form: Green ground tiles, clear visibility, sparse monster presence
Distinctive religious architecture with cross or altar imagery
safety_signals.meaning: Healing and resurrection services available; sanctuary

safety_signals.visual_form: Stationary NPCs at counters or specific shop locations
safety_signals.meaning: Commerce and rest available; neutral territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Characters standing motionless or in relaxed posture
safety_signals.meaning: No immediate threat; area is pacified

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue/light colors dominant in player sprite
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castles allied with player, friendly town banners

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark-colored minion sprites; demonic creature designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Charlock Castle; corrupted/barren landscape; shadow effects

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Purple-tinted monster sprites; magical effect overlays
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Demon realm aesthetics; magical corruption visual filters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark regions remain corrupted; castle inaccessible or heavily guarded; corrupted landscape tiles persist
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High encounter rate in villain-controlled zones; NPCs remain fearful; guards block certain passages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipment upgrades visible on player sprite; enhanced visual polish as story advances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Easier enemy encounters early game; dialogue from NPCs reflects awareness of player's deeds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark terrain may lighten; corrupted zones visually normalize; celebrating NPC animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced monster encounter frequency; NPCs show relief or celebration poses; passages previously blocked become accessible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desaturated or tinted environmental palette; status effect visual overlays on terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs move sluggishly or with distressed animations; special encounter tables active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprite presence in town; darker lighting; defensive structures visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs hide indoors; guards stand alert; combat encounters occur in normally-safe zones

environmental_storytelling.example: Early game towns have simple, modest buildings; later game towns show more ornate, hopeful architecture reflecting restored prosperity
Thriving towns have many NPCs in streets; towns under threat or curse show empty streets and barricaded buildings
Villain's territories surrounded by dense monster spawns; cleared areas show reduced encounter rates, indicating successful pacification
Dragonlord's castle uses dark stone and shadow; hero towns use bright, warm colors to contrast good vs. evil domains
Obtained keys, scrolls, and artifacts appear in player inventory/equipment visual representation, signaling progression
Impassable terrain or NPC guards block routes until story conditions met; visual removal signals world-state change

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Slow movement; appears in early grassy areas; low threat animation
Central arena position; dramatic sprite size increase; special attack animations with visual effects
Stands at gate or checkpoint; relaxed posture; allows passage when conditions met
Tense stance; blocks doorway; initiates combat if player attempts passage without authorization
Unusual animation loops; possibly erratic movement; visual status effect overlay; hostile behavior toward player
Stationary at altar; casting animation when player interacts; healing visual effects emit from sprite
Stands at counter; shows exchange animation when player trades; remains passive and non-hostile

notes: Dragon Quest I & II (SNES compilation) uses classic 16-bit RPG visual language: tile-based color palettes to communicate safety/danger, architectural design to signal faction/location type, and NPC animation states to reflect world conditions. The Dragonlord and Hargon/Malroth as antagonists are distinguished primarily through dark palette choices and demonic/magical visual motifs. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on terrain variation, NPC presence/absence, and monster encounter frequency rather than detailed environmental props.
